docs.title: Parts Diagram for Shopify
docs.text:
Turn flat product images into interactive diagrams. Shoppers click any labeled hotspot to see the matching variant, price, stock, and add it straight to their cart — without leaving the product page.

  • Get started in 5 minutes Install the app, upload an image, drop hotspots, and publish.

  • Admin UI Manage diagrams, assign them to products, tune the storefront, watch analytics.

  • Theme editor Add the Parts Diagram block to your theme. One setting — API base URL — that most stores can leave blank.

  • Storefront experience See how shoppers interact with the diagram on the live product page.

docs.title: What is Parts Diagram?
docs.text:
Parts Diagram is a Shopify app for stores that sell complex products with many parts, components, or accessories — bikes, machinery, tools, furniture, electronics, automotive parts.

Instead of forcing shoppers to scroll through a flat parts list and decode SKUs, you upload a single diagram image, draw a hotspot over each part, and link it to the matching Shopify product or variant. The storefront block renders an interactive overlay with prices, stock state, and inline add-to-cart for every hotspot.

docs.title: Three surfaces, one workflow
docs.text: Where you work What you do there Embedded admin (this app inside Shopify Admin) Upload diagrams, draw hotspots, map them to products/variants, tune storefront display, view analytics Theme editor (Shopify customize) Add the Parts Diagram block to your product template; pin per-environment API URL Storefront (live product page) What your customers see — interactive image, hover highlights, click-to-add-to-cart

docs.title: Quick links
docs.text:
• Install the app
  • Build your first diagram
  • Storefront settings reference
  • Troubleshooting
  • Changelog
  • Contact support

Live at sp-parts-diagram-docs.papathemes.com.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Releases of the Parts Diagram app, newest first. Items marked Storefront affect what shoppers see; Admin changes are visible inside the embedded admin only; Theme changes affect the theme editor block.

For the full developer-facing changelog, see the project repository's CHANGELOG.md.

docs.title: v1.0.0 — Initial release
docs.text:
Forthcoming — finalized at Shopify App Store approval.

This page is updated for every release. Subscribe to the support mailing list to get notified.

docs.title: Release-note template
docs.text: When new versions ship, entries follow this shape:

docs.title: vX.Y.Z — YYYY-MM-DD
docs.text:
Storefront

  • …

Admin

  • …

Theme

  • …

Fixed

  • …

Known issues

  • …

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Does Parts Diagram work with my theme?

Any Online Store 2.0 theme works. That includes every theme Shopify has published since mid-2021 (Dawn, Refresh, Sense, and every paid theme on the Shopify Theme Store).

Themes from before mid-2021 are usually not OS 2.0 and don't support Theme App Extensions.

Do I need a developer to install it?

No. Installation is the standard Shopify app flow: click Add app, approve scopes. Adding the block to your theme is a drag-and-drop step in the customize editor — no code edits.

Do I need to upload products into the app?

No. The app reads products and variants directly from your existing Shopify catalog. You only upload the diagram image itself.

docs.title: Diagrams & hotspots
docs.text:
How many hotspots can a diagram have?

There is no fixed limit. In practice, diagrams with 30+ hotspots work fine but become hard for shoppers to navigate visually. If your product genuinely has more parts than that, consider splitting into multiple diagrams (one per assembly) and assigning all of them to the same product.

Can a hotspot link to a product on another store?

No — only to products in the same Shopify store the app is installed on. Cross-store linking is on the roadmap but not available today.

Can a hotspot be linked to a collection or page?

Currently hotspots only support product and variant targets. Linking to collections, pages, or external URLs is on the roadmap.

What happens if I delete a product after mapping it?

The hotspot keeps its mapping but the storefront popup shows "currently unavailable". The admin dashboard surfaces a warning and the affected diagram is flagged as Has issues in the list.

docs.title: Storefront
docs.text:
Why don't I see prices on my live site?

Check Settings → Storefront → Show prices. If it's enabled and prices still don't appear, the most common cause is an unhealthy hotspot mapping — open the diagram editor and verify each hotspot has a current product and variant.

Why is the Add to cart button missing?

Either Show add-to-cart is off in Settings → Storefront, or the variant is sold out (in which case the button is replaced with Sold out state).

Why doesn't the variant picker appear in the popup?

Three possibilities:

  1. The hotspot is mapped to a specific variant, not the product — in which case there's no choice to make.
  2. The product only has the auto-default Default Title variant (i.e. it has no real variants). The picker is hidden in this case to avoid clutter.
  3. Show variant selector is off in the storefront settings.
Does it work on mobile?

Yes. The block uses responsive layouts: side panel collapses below the diagram on narrow screens, hotspots are tappable with thumbs, and the popup is sized for mobile use.

docs.title: Billing & data
docs.text:
Is there a free trial?

Yes. New installs include a trial — see Plans & billing. The remaining trial days appear at the top of the Billing screen.

What happens to my diagrams if I uninstall?

They are kept in cold storage for 30 days. Reinstalling within that window restores everything. After 30 days the data is permanently deleted.

Can I export my data?

Yes — see Import & Export. Diagrams export as JSON; assets export as a zip.

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
Will the block slow down my product page?

The block is loaded asynchronously and renders a placeholder skeleton until data arrives, so it doesn't block the rest of your page. Typical first paint is under 300 ms on warm cache.

Does the block affect Lighthouse scores?

Negligibly. The block follows Theme Store performance guidelines (CSS ≤ 100 KB, JS ≤ 16 KB, deferred loading) so Lighthouse scores on stores using the block remain in the green tier.

docs.title: Support
docs.text:
How do I contact support?

Email support@papathemes.com — see Support for response SLA and priority levels.

docs.text:
This documentation is made available exclusively to merchants and their designated staff who have a current paid or trial subscription to the Parts Diagram Shopify app published by Papathemes. Permitted use is limited to:

  • Reading the documentation in a web browser.
  • Following the instructions to operate, configure, and troubleshoot your own subscribed instance of the Parts Diagram app.
  • Quoting short excerpts (≤ 90 words) inside internal company documentation, support tickets, or training materials, with attribution to "Papathemes — Parts Diagram User Guide" and a link back to the relevant page.

docs.title: Support
docs.text: Need help? Reach the Parts Diagram team here.

docs.title: Email
docs.text:
support@papathemes.com

Please include:

  • Your Shopify store domain (e.g. your-store.myshopify.com).
  • A screenshot or short screen recording of the problem.
  • The affected product page URL, if relevant.
  • Any messages from the browser console starting with [parts-diagram] (DevTools → Console).

docs.title: Response SLA
docs.text:
Priority What it means First response P1 — Storefront down Block doesn't render or breaks checkout Within 4 business hours P2 — Storefront degraded Some hotspots / features broken Within 1 business day P3 — Admin issue Editor / settings issue with workarounds Within 2 business days P4 — Question / feature request Non-blocking Within 5 business days

Business hours are 9:00–17:00 UTC+7, Monday–Friday.

docs.title: Status & outages
docs.text:
For real-time service status (API, App Proxy, embedded admin), visit our status page:

status.parts-diagram.example.com

If you suspect an outage, check the status page first — we post incident updates there before email.

docs.title: Feature requests
docs.text:
We track requests and prioritize by store impact. To submit one, email support with:

  • The use case ("we sell X and our customers struggle to do Y").
  • The change you'd like.
  • Whether a workaround exists today.

We don't promise timelines on feature requests, but every request is read and triaged.

docs.title: Privacy & security
docs.text: Security issues — please report privately to security@papathemes.com rather than the standard support address. We acknowledge security reports within 24 hours.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Quick lookup for common issues. Theme-editor-specific problems live in Theme editor → Troubleshooting.

docs.title: The block doesn't render at all
docs.text: Symptom Likely cause Fix Block area is blank, no skeleton Block isn't placed on this product template Add the block to the right template Skeleton appears, never resolves API call failing Open DevTools → Network → look for /apps/parts-diagram/* errors. Share the error code with support <noscript> message visible JavaScript disabled in the browser Tell the shopper to enable JS — fundamental requirement Looks fine in incognito but not in normal browser Browser extension (ad-blocker, privacy tool) blocking Test with extensions disabled

docs.title: Hotspots are misaligned
docs.text: Symptom Cause Fix All hotspots offset by a fixed amount Image was replaced with one of different proportions Re-upload the original image, or redraw hotspots on the new one Hotspots drift on resize Theme is wrapping the diagram in a container with non-stretching layout Contact support — usually a CSS conflict we can fix Hotspots correct on desktop, off on mobile Theme injects responsive transforms Same as above

docs.title: Pricing or stock looks wrong
docs.text: Symptom Cause Fix Old price flashes briefly before correct one Snapshot vs. live data — first paint uses snapshot Expected behavior; if persistent, re-save the hotspot mapping in the editor Prices missing entirely Show prices off, or hotspot has no variant mapping Check Settings → Storefront and the diagram editor Stock state stuck on "Sold out" but variant is in stock Inventory tracking edge case Reload page; if persistent, contact support Sale price not showing struck-through Variant has no compare_at_price, or it's lower than price Set compare_at_price higher than price on the variant in Shopify Admin

docs.title: Variant picker problems
docs.text: Symptom Cause Fix Variant picker not appearing Hotspot locked to single variant, or product only has Default Title variant Open the editor and verify the hotspot's variant field is "Any variant"; verify the product has real options Picker shows but variant change doesn't update price/stock Cached storefront data Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R / Cmd+Shift+R) Picker shows raw "Default Title" option Product is missing variant configuration in Shopify Configure variants on the product in Shopify Admin

docs.title: Add to cart not working
docs.text: Symptom Cause Fix Click does nothing, no error Theme is overriding cart events Test on a Dawn-based theme to isolate; share theme name with support "Variant unavailable" error Variant was deleted between page load and click Reload the page Item adds but cart count doesn't update Theme uses a non-standard cart UI Set Cart redirect mode to Open cart drawer or Go to cart page

docs.title: Admin UI issues
docs.text: Symptom Cause Fix Embedded admin shows blank screen Shopify session expired Refresh the Shopify Admin page "Block missing" warning, but block is added Shop-level health check is stale Click Re-check on the dashboard Hotspot save fails repeatedly Rate-limit or API outage Wait a minute and retry; persistent failures → contact support Image upload fails for large file File over 5 MB Compress the image (e.g. with pngcrush or an online tool)

docs.title: Still stuck?
docs.text:
If your issue isn't above:

  1. Capture a screenshot showing the problem.
  2. Open browser DevTools → Console, copy any messages starting with [parts-diagram] or anything red/yellow.
  3. Note the affected product page URL.
  4. Email all three to support.

docs.title: Admin UI
docs.text:
Everything you do inside Shopify Admin — managing diagrams, assigning them, configuring the storefront, watching analytics.

The admin embeds inside the standard Shopify Admin chrome, so it inherits your account permissions, polaris navigation, and dark/light theme.

docs.title: Map of the admin
docs.text: Area What it's for Dashboard At-a-glance metrics, recent activity, links into common workflows Diagrams → Create Upload an image and create a diagram entry Diagrams → Editor Draw hotspots, map them to products and variants, set labels Diagrams → List Search, filter, duplicate, archive existing diagrams Diagrams → Import & Export Move diagrams between stores via JSON Assignments Tie a diagram to one or more products Product Diagrams Per-product view of which diagrams render where Storefront Preview Verify changes before they hit shoppers Analytics Hotspot impressions, clicks, add-to-cart, conversion Settings → Storefront Layout, hover, prices, SKU, ATC visibility, swatches Settings → Shop Shop-wide preferences Settings → Billing Subscription, usage caps, plan changes

docs.title: Source-of-truth rules
docs.text:
A few choices the app makes are worth knowing up front:

Storefront display lives in Settings → Storefront, not the theme editor

Things like layout, hover effect, "show prices", "show SKU", "show variant selector", and "show add-to-cart" are configured once, in Settings → Storefront, and apply to every block on the storefront.

The Parts Diagram block in the theme editor exposes only the API base URL — by design. This avoids the "ghost control" problem where two settings disagree and one silently wins.

See Storefront settings for the full list of toggles.

docs.title: Analytics
docs.text:
Understand what shoppers actually click, and where Parts Diagram is moving the needle on your storefront.

Open via Analytics in the sidebar.

docs.title: Top-level metrics
docs.text:
• Hotspot impressions — number of times the diagram was rendered to a shopper.
  • Hotspot interactions — hovers and clicks across all hotspots.
  • Add-to-cart events — items added to cart via the inline Parts Diagram popup.
  • Conversion — share of sessions with a hotspot interaction that ended in an add-to-cart.

Each metric shows the change vs. the previous equivalent period.

docs.title: Time range & comparisons
docs.text:
• Pick a preset range: Today, Last 7 days, Last 30 days, Last 90 days, Custom.
  • Toggle Compare to previous period to overlay the previous range on the trend chart.

The retention window depends on your plan — see Plans & billing.

docs.title: Per-diagram breakdown
docs.text:
Below the headline metrics, a table lists every active diagram with:

  • Impressions, interactions, and add-to-cart counts.
  • Click-through rate (interactions / impressions).
  • Conversion rate (add-to-cart / interactions).
  • Top three hotspots by clicks.

Click any diagram name to drill into a per-hotspot view: which parts shoppers are most interested in. Use this to spot:

  • Hotspots no one clicks (label may be unclear, or the part isn't relevant to shoppers).
  • Hotspots with high clicks but low add-to-cart (price, stock, or the popup UX may be the friction).

docs.title: Per-product breakdown
docs.text:
A second table flips the view: which products generate the most interaction. Use this to identify candidates for:

  • Promoting in marketing — they're already drawing attention.
  • Adding more diagrams or richer mappings — shoppers are engaged here.

docs.title: Export
docs.text: Click Export CSV to download the raw data for the current range and filter set. Columns include date, diagram, hotspot, product, variant, event type, count.

docs.title: Privacy
docs.text: Analytics are aggregated server-side. No personally identifiable information about shoppers is collected. Sessions are deduplicated by a short-lived first-party cookie that respects browser do-not-track preferences.

docs.title: Assignments
docs.text:
A diagram only renders on the storefront when it's assigned to a product. Assignments tell the Parts Diagram block: "on this product page, show this diagram".

Open via Assignments in the sidebar.

docs.title: How assignments work
docs.text:
• A single product can have multiple diagrams attached. They render in the order shown — drag to reorder.
  • A single diagram can be attached to multiple products — handy for product families that share the same parts illustration.
  • Assignments persist across theme switches; they're stored on the app, not on the theme.

docs.title: Create an assignment
docs.text:
1. Click Add assignment.
  2. Pick the product with the search box (matches by title, SKU, handle).
  3. Pick the diagram to attach.
  4. Optionally set a display name override — what the diagram tab is labeled when multiple diagrams are attached to the same product.
  5. Save.

The storefront reflects the change immediately on next page load.

docs.title: Manage existing assignments
docs.text:
Each row shows:

  • Product (with thumbnail).
  • Diagram name.
  • Position (when more than one diagram is attached).
  • Last modified.

Row actions:

  • Open product — jump to its Product Diagrams view.
  • Open diagram — jump to the editor.
  • Reorder — drag to change which diagram appears first when a product has multiple.
  • Remove — detach the diagram from this product. The diagram is not deleted; it can still be assigned to other products.

docs.title: Bulk assignments
docs.text:
For catalogs with many similar products:

  1. Select multiple products with checkboxes (filter by collection, vendor, or product type to narrow the list).
  2. Click Bulk assign and choose a diagram.
  3. Confirm. The same diagram is attached to every selected product.

This is the fastest way to roll a single diagram across an entire collection.

docs.title: Edge cases
docs.text:
• Deleted product — assignments to deleted products surface as a warning on the Dashboard. Either re-create the product or remove the assignment.
  • Draft / unpublished product — assignments work, but the diagram only renders on storefront pages your shoppers can actually reach.
  • Variant-level mapping — assignments are at product level. Use the diagram editor to pin individual hotspots to specific variants.

docs.title: Billing
docs.text: Manage your subscription, see usage against plan caps, and switch plans. Open via Settings → Billing.

docs.title: What's on the screen
docs.text:
• Current plan — name, price, included caps.
  • Trial status — remaining trial days if applicable.
  • Usage this period — diagrams active, hotspot views, analytics retention used.
  • Plan options — list of all plans with Switch buttons.
  • Invoices — link to your Shopify Admin billing screen, where the app's charges appear alongside your other Shopify invoices.

docs.title: Switching plans
docs.text:
1. Pick the target plan.
  2. Click Switch. Shopify presents its standard subscription approval dialog.
  3. Approve.
  4. The new caps apply immediately. Shopify prorates the change on your next invoice.

Downgrading to a plan with lower caps that you currently exceed prompts you to reduce usage first (e.g. archive diagrams) — the downgrade can't proceed until you're within the new caps.

docs.title: Cancel
docs.text:
Uninstalling the app from Shopify Admin cancels the subscription automatically. There's no separate Cancel button inside the app — this is by Shopify's design.

After uninstall, your data is retained for 30 days. Reinstall within that window to restore everything.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Plans & billing overview
  • Shopify's App refund policy

docs.title: Dashboard
docs.text: The first screen after you open the app. Use it to check store health and jump into common workflows.

docs.title: What's on the dashboard
docs.text:
• Headline metrics — diagrams created, products assigned, hotspot impressions in the current period, add-to-cart events from hotspots.
  • Recent diagrams — last few diagrams you edited, with a click to resume editing.
  • Quick actions — "New diagram", "New assignment", "Open theme editor".
  • Health checks — alerts if any product is missing its assigned diagram, if hotspots reference deleted variants, or if your storefront block is missing from the active theme.

docs.title: Health checks in detail
docs.text: Status Meaning Fix All clear No issues detected Nothing to do Block missing on theme The Parts Diagram block isn't placed on the active theme's product template Add the block Hotspot maps to deleted variant A hotspot references a variant that no longer exists Open the diagram editor and re-map or remove the hotspot Diagram missing image The image asset for a diagram failed to upload or was deleted Open the diagram and re-upload No diagrams yet New install — get started by creating one Your first diagram

docs.title: Tip
docs.text: If you have many diagrams, pin the dashboard tab in your browser — the metrics surface refreshes hourly and is the fastest way to confirm everything is healthy after a theme update or product import.

docs.title: Create a Diagram
docs.text: A diagram is a single image with a set of hotspots layered on top. This page covers creating the diagram entry and uploading the image — hotspot drawing happens in the Diagram editor.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. From the sidebar, click Diagrams → New diagram.
  2. Enter a name. This is internal only — shoppers never see it. Pick something you'll recognize in the diagram list (e.g. "2024 Snowboard Mounting Plate").
  3. Optionally add internal notes for your team. Useful when multiple staff members maintain the catalog.
  4. Drop or browse to your image file.
  5. Click Save. The app uploads the image and creates the diagram.

You'll be taken straight into the editor to start drawing hotspots.

docs.title: Image requirements
docs.text:
Property Recommendation Format PNG (preferred, supports transparency) or JPG Width 1200 – 2400 px Height Whatever fits the layout — not constrained File size Up to 5 MB Color profile sRGB Background White or transparent works best — heavily textured backgrounds make hotspots hard to see

Keep one source of truth

If you re-export your diagram artwork, replace the image on the same diagram entry rather than creating a new one. All existing hotspots stay anchored to their normalized coordinates and continue to work as long as the proportions are similar.

docs.title: What happens behind the scenes
docs.text:
• The image is stored in object storage (S3) behind a private CDN URL.
  • The diagram metadata (name, dimensions, hotspot list) is stored in the app database.
  • Nothing is published to the storefront yet — you still need to draw hotspots, map them, and assign the diagram to a product.

docs.title: Diagram Editor
docs.text: The editor is where you transform a flat image into an interactive diagram. Open it by clicking any diagram from the list, or by clicking Save on the create screen.

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
The editor is a two-column workspace:

  • Canvas (left) — your diagram image with the hotspot overlay.
  • Inspector (right) — properties of the currently selected hotspot: label, mapped product, mapped variant, custom display title.

A toolbar at the top of the canvas switches between modes:

Mode Shortcut What it does Select V Move and resize existing hotspots Draw rectangle R Click-and-drag a rectangle hotspot Draw point P Click to drop a point hotspot Pan Space (hold) Drag the canvas Zoom scroll Zoom canvas under cursor

docs.title: Rectangle hotspots
docs.text:
Best for parts with a clear bounding box (a wheel, a button, a panel).

  1. Press R or click the rectangle tool.
  2. Click-and-drag from one corner to the opposite corner of the part.
  3. Release to commit. The hotspot is selected and its inspector opens.

docs.title: Point hotspots
docs.text:
Best for small parts where a rectangle would feel cluttered (a screw, a connector, an indicator light).

  1. Press P or click the point tool.
  2. Click the location once. The point is dropped and selected.

docs.title: Editing a hotspot
docs.text:
• Drag the body to move.
  • Drag a corner handle to resize (rectangles only).
  • Press Delete or Backspace to remove the selected hotspot.
  • Use Cmd/Ctrl + Z / Shift + Cmd/Ctrl + Z for undo / redo.

docs.title: Mapping a hotspot to a product
docs.text:
This is what makes the hotspot interactive on the storefront.

In the inspector for the selected hotspot:

  1. Use the Product picker to search by title or SKU. Pick the matching Shopify product.
  2. If the product has variants, the Variant dropdown becomes active. Pick the specific variant the hotspot represents.
  3. Leave it on "Any variant" if the hotspot represents the product as a whole and shoppers should see the variant picker in the popup.
  4. Pick a single variant to lock the hotspot to that exact SKU — no picker shown in the popup.

The app records a snapshot (price, compare-at price, image, SKU) at mapping time, so the storefront can render an accurate first paint even before live data loads.

docs.title: Labels and display titles
docs.text:
Field Where it shows When to fill in Label Hover tooltip on the image; bold title in the side panel and popup Always recommended — short and friendly ("Toe buckle", not "TC-447-A") Custom display title Overrides the product name shown in the popup body Rarely needed. Use it when the Shopify product name is too long or technical to surface to shoppers

Label takes priority over product title

If a hotspot has a label, the storefront shows the label as the bold title for that part. If the label is empty, the storefront falls back to the underlying product's title. This keeps the side panel readable even when many parts share the same base product.

docs.title: Saving
docs.text: The editor auto-saves every few seconds while you work. The header shows a Saved indicator after each change. There's no separate "publish" step — once a hotspot is saved on a diagram that's assigned to a product, it's live on the storefront.

docs.title: Keyboard shortcuts (full list)
docs.text: Action Shortcut Select tool V Rectangle tool R Point tool P Pan canvas hold Space and drag Undo / Redo Cmd/Ctrl + Z / Shift + Cmd/Ctrl + Z Delete hotspot Delete or Backspace Zoom in / out scroll wheel Fit to canvas 0 (zero) 100% zoom 1

docs.title: Diagram List
docs.text: The diagram list is the home base for everything you've built. Open it from Diagrams in the sidebar.

docs.title: What's on the list
docs.text:
Each row shows:

  • Thumbnail of the diagram image.
  • Name.
  • Hotspot count — how many parts the diagram contains.
  • Assigned products — count of products this diagram is currently attached to (click the number to jump to Assignments filtered to this diagram).
  • Status — one of:
    • Live — at least one assigned product is published, hotspots resolve to valid variants.
    • Unassigned — diagram has hotspots but isn't attached to any product.
    • Has issues — hotspots reference deleted products / variants. Open the editor to fix.
    • Archived — hidden from default view, not rendered on storefront.
  • Last updated date.

docs.title: Search and filter
docs.text:
• Search box — matches by diagram name and internal notes.
  • Status filter — show only Live / Unassigned / Has issues / Archived.
  • Sort — by name, last updated, hotspot count, assigned product count.

docs.title: Row actions
docs.text:
Hover any row to reveal:

  • Open — go to the editor.
  • Duplicate — clone the diagram with all hotspots; the copy is unassigned and named <original> (copy).
  • Assign — open the assignment dialog directly from the list.
  • Archive / Restore — archived diagrams don't render on the storefront and are excluded from the default view.

docs.title: Bulk actions
docs.text:
Select multiple rows with the leftmost checkboxes to:

  • Archive / Restore in bulk.
  • Export the selected diagrams as JSON (Import & Export).
  • Delete permanently (with confirmation — irreversible).

Delete vs Archive

Archive hides a diagram from active use but keeps the data and any history. Delete removes everything: hotspots, image, analytics history. Use Archive unless you're certain.

docs.title: Import & Export
docs.text:
Move diagrams between stores or back them up offline. The app exchanges diagrams as a single JSON file containing all hotspots, labels, mapped product handles, and image references.

Open via Diagrams → Import & Export.

docs.title: Export
docs.text:
1. Pick one or more diagrams from the list (or Export all).
  2. Click Export. The browser downloads a .json file.
  3. Optionally also download the image assets as a separate .zip.

The JSON file includes:

  • Diagram name and dimensions.
  • Hotspot list with normalized coordinates, labels, and product handles.
  • Variant mapping snapshots (price, SKU, title) at export time.

It does not include:

  • Live storefront analytics.
  • Internal team notes (these are per-store).
  • The image asset itself (download separately as the .zip).

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
1. Click Import, drop the JSON file (and optional asset .zip).
  2. The dialog previews the diagrams and reports:
    • Hotspots whose product handles match existing products on this store.
    • Hotspots whose handles don't match — you can manually remap them now or leave them blank to fix later in the editor.
  3. Click Import.

Imported diagrams are created as new entries — never overwriting existing ones — and start as Unassigned.

docs.title: Use cases
docs.text:
Situation Approach Migrating a diagram from a dev store to production Export from dev (with assets), Import on prod, assign to the same products Backing up before a large catalog edit Export all, store the JSON in your team's drive Building diagrams in a sandbox and shipping to the live store Same workflow — export + import

Variant snapshots may go stale

Exported snapshots reflect prices and stock at the moment of export. On the destination store, the Parts Diagram block always reads live Shopify data on the storefront, so the snapshot only affects the initial paint. If the destination store has very different prices, expect a flash of imported data before the live values arrive.

docs.title: Product Diagrams
docs.text:
The per-product view: which diagrams render on this product page, in what order, and whether all hotspots are healthy.

You arrive here either by clicking a product from the Assignments list, or via the global product picker in the sidebar.

docs.title: What's on the screen
docs.text:
• Product summary — title, thumbnail, status (Active / Draft / Archived on Shopify), variant count.
  • Storefront preview link — opens the live product page in a new tab so you can verify changes without manually navigating.
  • Attached diagrams — ordered list, drag to reorder.
  • Health indicators per attached diagram:
    • Healthy — all hotspots map to live products / variants.
    • N issues — some hotspots map to deleted entities. Click to drill into the diagram editor.
    • No image — diagram is missing its image asset.

docs.title: Reorder diagrams on this product
docs.text: Drag the diagrams in the Attached diagrams list. Order on the storefront updates on next page load — most stores show a tab bar across the top of the diagram block when more than one is attached.

docs.title: Detach a diagram
docs.text: Click the × next to any attached diagram. The diagram is unattached from this product only; it remains usable on other products.

docs.title: Attach another diagram
docs.text: Click Attach diagram and pick from the list. Same flow as Assignments, scoped to the current product.

docs.title: Jump into the editor
docs.text: Click the diagram name to open the Diagram editor with the current hotspot selection ready.

docs.title: Tips for large catalogs
docs.text:
• Use Storefront Preview before changing anything live to confirm the current state.
  • Pin frequently-edited products as bookmarks — Product Diagrams pages are the fastest entry point during catalog maintenance.
  • For products that share a diagram, edit the diagram itself — every attached product picks up the change immediately.

docs.title: Shop Settings
docs.text: Store-wide preferences that aren't tied to the storefront visual layer. Open via Settings → Shop.

docs.title: Localization
docs.text:
• Currency — confirms the currency the app reads from your Shopify store. Read-only; change it in Shopify Admin if you need to.
  • Number formatting — locale used to format prices in the diagram block. Defaults to the store's primary locale.

docs.title: Cart behavior
docs.text:
• Cart redirect mode — when a shopper clicks Add to cart in the hotspot popup:
    • Stay on page (default) — item is added silently and the cart count in the header updates.
    • Open cart drawer — Shopify's cart drawer opens (if your theme supports it).
    • Go to cart page — full navigation to /cart.
  • Cart line attributes — the app tags every line item it adds with _parts_diagram_source so you can filter cart analytics by traffic origin.

docs.title: Team & permissions
docs.text: • Staff who can edit diagrams — read-only list of staff accounts with the right Shopify permission. Permissions are managed in Shopify Admin under Settings → Users and permissions, not here.

docs.title: Data retention
docs.text:
• Analytics retention — read-only; reflects your current plan. See Plans & billing.
  • Soft-delete window — uninstalled stores are kept for 30 days so reinstall is non-destructive.

docs.title: Reset
docs.text: • Reset storefront settings to defaults — restores Storefront settings to factory defaults. Confirmation required; cannot be undone except by manually setting values back.

docs.title: Storefront Settings
docs.text:
The single source of truth for everything visual on the storefront block. Open via Settings → Storefront.

These settings override the theme editor

Layout, hover, prices, SKU visibility, variant selector, and add-to-cart visibility all live here. The Parts Diagram block in the theme editor exposes only the per-deployment API base URL. This avoids two-source-of-truth confusion.

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
The overall arrangement of the diagram and its parts list.

Layout Best for Side panel (default) Wide product pages — diagram on the left, scrollable parts list on the right Overlay Narrow pages or mobile-heavy stores — parts list overlays the image, with hotspot click expanding inline detail Popup-only Minimal layouts — diagram only, parts surface in a modal popup on click

The chosen layout applies to every diagram on the storefront.

docs.title: Hover effect
docs.text:
Controls how a hotspot reacts when a shopper points at it (or its matching parts-list row).

  • Highlight (default) — a colored outline pulses around the hotspot, and the side-panel row scrolls into view.
  • Spotlight — surrounding image area dims; the hotspot stays fully bright.
  • Off — no visual change on hover. Click is still active.

docs.title: Display toggles
docs.text: Toggle Default What it controls Show prices On Show variant price next to each part. When the variant has a compare_at_price higher than the live price, both show — sale price first, regular struck through. Show SKU On Show the variant SKU as a small caption under the part name. Show variant selector On When a hotspot maps to a product (not a specific variant), show the variant picker inside the popup. Hotspots locked to a single variant ignore this — they never show a picker. Show add-to-cart On Render the inline Add to cart button in the popup. When off, clicking a hotspot opens the popup but shoppers must navigate to the product page to buy. Show hotspot numbers On Render small numeric badges on each hotspot, useful when the diagram has a printed legend in the image itself.

docs.title: Variant swatches
docs.text:
When a hotspot popup shows a variant picker:

  • Single-variant products — variant picker is hidden when the only variant is the auto-default Default Title.
  • Color / size swatches — if your variants use option names like Color or Material, the picker renders as visual swatches. Otherwise it falls back to a dropdown.

docs.title: Sale & out-of-stock behavior
docs.text:
• When a variant has compare_at_price > price, the side-panel and popup show the sale price with the regular price struck through.
  • When a variant is sold out, the quantity field and the Add to cart button are disabled, and the inline state reads Sold out.

These behaviors are always on — there are no toggles for them.

docs.title: Saving
docs.text: Settings save to the app and apply on next storefront page load — no theme republish needed. Use Storefront Preview to verify changes before they reach shoppers.

docs.title: Storefront Preview
docs.text:
A sandboxed preview of how the Parts Diagram block looks on a product page — without having to publish changes to your live theme first.

Open via Storefront Preview in the sidebar.

docs.title: When to use it
docs.text:
• After tweaking Storefront settings — see the layout and toggles in action.
  • After remapping a hotspot — confirm the variant selector shows the right options.
  • Before bulk-assigning a diagram across a collection — preview on one product first.
  • When troubleshooting — the preview runs against the same data the storefront uses but inside the admin, so it's easier to inspect.

docs.title: What the preview is (and isn't)
docs.text:
The preview renders the storefront block in an iframe inside the admin, loading:

  • The current diagram for the selected product.
  • The latest Storefront settings (no theme publish needed).
  • The same live Shopify pricing / stock data the real storefront uses.

It does not:

  • Run inside your actual theme template — so theme-specific styles (custom fonts, headers, etc.) are not applied.
  • Charge any real cart actions. Adding to cart inside the preview shows the success state but doesn't post to your store's cart.
  • Track analytics events (preview sessions are excluded from the analytics dashboard).

docs.title: Using the preview
docs.text:
1. Pick the product with the picker at the top.
  2. The preview iframe loads with the assigned diagram(s).
  3. Toggle the Layout dropdown to compare side / overlay / popup variants without committing the change to settings.
  4. Click hotspots to see the popup behavior, variant picker, and add-to-cart UI exactly as a shopper would.

When you're satisfied, save the change in the relevant settings page — the preview is read-only: it never writes to your store.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Three short reads to take you from zero to a published diagram on a live product page.

  1. Install the app — one-time setup per store.
  2. Your first diagram — upload, draw hotspots, map variants, embed.
  3. Plans & billing — what's included at each tier.

Already installed?

Skip ahead to Your first diagram.

docs.title: Plans & Billing
docs.text:
Parts Diagram bills through Shopify's standard subscription system. Charges appear on your monthly Shopify invoice — no separate payment method is required.

Plan tiers

Pricing details are finalized at app publish time. The tier limits below describe the shape of the plans; actual numbers will be reflected in the Billing screen inside the embedded admin.

docs.title: What's included
docs.text: Feature All plans Unlimited diagrams Variant + product mapping Storefront block (Theme App Extension) Storefront layouts (side / overlay / popup) Inline add-to-cart Sale price display Out-of-stock state handling Email support

docs.title: What scales by plan
docs.text:
Higher tiers raise these caps:

  • Active diagrams — the maximum number of diagrams that can be simultaneously assigned to live products.
  • Monthly hotspot views — total hotspot impressions counted across all storefront sessions in the current billing period.
  • Analytics retention — how far back you can scroll the analytics dashboard.
  • Concurrent admin seats — number of staff accounts that can edit diagrams at the same time.

docs.title: How to change plan
docs.text:
1. In the embedded admin, open Settings → Billing.
  2. Pick the new plan, click Switch.
  3. Shopify presents its standard subscription approval screen.
  4. After you approve, the new limits take effect immediately and your next Shopify invoice prorates the change.

docs.title: Cancel or uninstall
docs.text:
Uninstalling the app from Shopify Admin automatically cancels the subscription — you won't be charged again after the next invoice.

Your diagrams and assignments are kept in cold storage for 30 days after uninstall. Reinstall within that window and everything is restored. After 30 days, data is permanently deleted.

docs.title: Trial & refunds
docs.text:
• Every new install starts with a free trial. The remaining trial days are visible at the top of the Billing screen.
  • Refunds are handled by Shopify per their standard App refund policy.

docs.title: Your First Diagram
docs.text: A complete walkthrough — from blank slate to a live, clickable diagram on your storefront. Allow ~5 minutes if your products and a diagram image are already prepared.

docs.title: What you need
docs.text:
• One diagram image — a flat illustration or photo showing the parts you want to make clickable. PNG or JPG, ideally at least 1200 px wide.
  • The Shopify products or variants each part should link to. They must already exist in your catalog (the app does not create products).

docs.title: 1. Create the diagram
docs.text:
1. In the embedded admin, click Diagrams → New diagram.
  2. Give it a name (this is internal — shoppers never see it).
  3. Drop in your diagram image. Wait for the upload to confirm.

Image guidelines

  • Background: solid white or transparent works best.
  • Resolution: aim for ~1.5–2× the largest size you'll display.
  • Layout: leave breathing room around parts so hotspots don't crowd labels.

docs.title: 2. Draw hotspots
docs.text:
In the editor, switch to Draw mode and click-and-drag a rectangle (or click to drop a point) over each part you want to make interactive.

Each hotspot needs:

  • A label (optional but recommended — shown to shoppers when hovering and inside the popup header).
  • A mapped product — pick a Shopify product, then optionally a specific variant.

You can edit hotspot positions, resize them, or remove them at any time.

docs.title: 3. Assign the diagram to a product
docs.text:
A diagram becomes visible on the storefront only when it's assigned to the product whose product page should display it.

  1. Open Assignments in the sidebar.
  2. Click Add assignment and pick the target product.
  3. Choose the diagram from the list.
  4. Save.

You can assign the same diagram to multiple products.

docs.title: 4. Add the block to your theme
docs.text:
The first time you set up Parts Diagram on a theme:

  1. Open Online Store → Themes → Customize.
  2. Switch the top selector to a Product template.
  3. In the section list, click Add block under the product information section, then choose Parts Diagram under "Apps".
  4. Drag the block to where you want it (most stores place it directly below the product gallery).
  5. Click Save.

Full theme-editor walkthrough →

docs.title: 5. Verify on the storefront
docs.text:
Open the live product page in a regular browser tab. You should see:

  • The diagram image rendered with a side panel of clickable parts.
  • Hovering a hotspot highlights both the image area and the matching side-panel row.
  • Clicking a hotspot opens a popup with the part's price, stock, and variant picker (if applicable), plus an inline Add to cart button.

If anything is missing, jump to Troubleshooting.

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Tune the storefront look and feel: Storefront settings
  • Watch what shoppers actually click: Analytics
  • Move bulk diagrams between stores: Import & Export

docs.title: Install the App
docs.text: Parts Diagram is installed once per store, the same way as any other Shopify app.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• A Shopify store on any plan that supports custom apps (Basic and above).
  • A theme on Online Store 2.0 (every theme published in the Shopify Theme Store after mid-2021 qualifies — including Dawn, Refresh, Sense, and most paid themes). The app embeds via Theme App Extensions, which require OS 2.0.
  • Permission to install apps and edit the theme on that store.

docs.title: Install steps
docs.text:
1. Open the Parts Diagram listing on the Shopify App Store (link will be added when the listing is approved).
  2. Click Add app, then Install app.
  3. Review the requested scopes — read access to products and themes, write access to your storefront via the App Proxy. Approve.
  4. After Shopify finishes installing, you land on the embedded admin inside Shopify Admin.

Sandbox / development install

If you're piloting Parts Diagram on a development store before the public listing is live, use the install URL we provided you. The flow is identical — Shopify still walks you through scope approval.

docs.title: What changes on your store
docs.text:
After install:

  • A new app entry Parts Diagram appears under Apps in your Shopify Admin sidebar.
  • A new theme block Parts Diagram becomes available in the theme editor (under "Apps" in any product section).
  • A subscription is created against your store but not charged until you select a plan. See Plans & billing.

docs.title: Next: build your first diagram
docs.text: Your first diagram

docs.title: Storefront Experience
docs.text:
What your customers see. Use these pages as a reference when explaining the feature to your team, support staff, or marketing.

Page Covers How it works Shopper journey from page load to add-to-cart Layouts Side / overlay / popup layouts compared Interactions Hover, click, variant select, add-to-cart inline Accessibility Keyboard navigation, screen reader behavior, contrast

For the toggles that control these behaviors, see Storefront settings.

docs.title: Accessibility
docs.text: The Parts Diagram block is built to meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards. This page documents the assistive-technology behavior so you can answer accessibility audits with confidence.

docs.title: Keyboard navigation
docs.text:
• All interactive elements are reachable with Tab in a predictable order.
  • Tab order: diagram tabs → side-panel rows → open popup (variant picker → quantity → add-to-cart) → close button.
  • Enter or Space activates focused rows and buttons.
  • Esc closes the popup.
  • Focus is trapped inside the popup while it's open — Tab cycles within the popup and does not leak to the page underneath.
  • Focus is restored to the originating hotspot row when the popup closes.

docs.title: Screen reader behavior
docs.text:
• The diagram image carries an alt attribute equal to its name.
  • Each hotspot is exposed as a button with an accessible name made from its label and product title (e.g. "Toe buckle, 1 of 8").
  • Side-panel rows are also button elements with the same naming pattern.
  • Popups announce as a dialog. The dialog title is the part name; the variant picker announces option name and value changes.
  • Stock state is announced — a sold-out hotspot reads as "Toe buckle, sold out" so blind shoppers don't waste time interacting with unavailable variants.

docs.title: Color contrast
docs.text:
All interactive text and controls in the block meet the WCAG AA contrast minimum (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for UI components). The hover highlight, focus ring, and price strikethrough all pass against the standard light and dark theme palettes.

If your theme uses unusually low-contrast colors, the block falls back to its own neutral palette to maintain readability.

docs.title: Motion
docs.text: The hover effect uses a short, low-amplitude pulse animation. Shoppers who have prefers-reduced-motion set in their OS see the highlight without animation — the color change is applied instantly.

docs.title: Visible focus
docs.text: Focus rings are visible and respect the theme's :focus-visible styling when the theme defines one. A neutral high-contrast ring is applied as fallback when the theme provides no rule.

docs.title: What we don't yet support
docs.text:
• Voice control for hotspot navigation by name. Voice users can reach hotspots via the side-panel rows, which are standard buttons, but there's no shortcut to address a hotspot by label alone.
  • Custom keyboard shortcuts inside the storefront popup. Standard Tab / Enter / Esc only.

If you need to extend accessibility behavior for your store's audience, contact support — we triage accessibility requests as priority bugs.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text: A walkthrough of the storefront experience as a shopper sees it, from page load to add-to-cart.

docs.title: 1. Page load
docs.text:
A shopper lands on a product page that has at least one diagram assigned. The Parts Diagram block:

  • Renders a placeholder skeleton inside its assigned section so the page layout stabilizes immediately.
  • Reads the product ID from the page and asks the API for the diagram(s) attached to that product.
  • Loads the storefront settings (layout, hover, display toggles) from the same API call.
  • Replaces the skeleton with the live diagram once data arrives, typically in under 300 ms on a warm cache.

docs.title: 2. First paint
docs.text:
The shopper sees:

  • The diagram image.
  • Numeric badges (1, 2, 3, …) on each hotspot, if hotspot numbers are enabled.
  • A side panel — or overlay, depending on layout — listing every hotspot with its label, price, and SKU.

If the diagram contains many parts, the side panel scrolls independently of the page.

docs.title: 3. Hover / focus
docs.text:
Pointing at a hotspot or its side-panel row:

  • Highlights both — the hotspot pulses or spotlights (depending on the hover effect setting), and the matching side-panel row gets a colored border.
  • Surfaces the part's label as a tooltip on the image.

Keyboard users can Tab through the side-panel rows; the same highlight pairing applies.

docs.title: 4. Click
docs.text:
Clicking a hotspot or its side-panel row opens the product popup:

  • The popup header shows the part's number badge.
  • The popup body shows:
    • Bold title (the hotspot's label, falling back to the product title if no label is set).
    • Thumbnail (clickable — links to the underlying product page).
    • Sale price + regular price struck through, if on sale.
    • SKU (if enabled).
    • Variant picker (if the hotspot is mapped to a product, not a specific variant, and the product has more than the auto-default variant).
    • Quantity stepper.
    • Add to cart button (or Sold out when stock = 0).

Clicking the thumbnail or the bold title navigates to the underlying Shopify product page in a new tab.

docs.title: 5. Add to cart
docs.text:
Clicking Add to cart in the popup:

  • Sends a Shopify cart request via the standard Cart API.
  • Shows a success state on the button.
  • Updates the cart count in the theme's header.
  • Optionally opens the cart drawer or navigates to /cart, depending on the shop setting.

The popup stays open so shoppers can add multiple parts in succession without re-finding them on the diagram.

docs.title: Edge cases
docs.text:
• Sold-out variant — quantity stepper and add-to-cart button are disabled; the popup reads Sold out.
  • Product missing — if the hotspot's mapped product was deleted on Shopify after the diagram was edited, the hotspot still renders but the popup shows a "This part is currently unavailable" message. This case is also flagged on the admin dashboard.
  • No JavaScript — the block renders a <noscript> fallback asking the shopper to enable JavaScript. The diagram is JS-driven and cannot fall back to a static list.

For accessibility specifics, see Accessibility.

docs.title: Interactions
docs.text: Detailed reference for every shopper interaction the block supports.

docs.title: Hover (mouse)
docs.text:
Pointing at a hotspot on the image:

  • Triggers the chosen hover effect: highlight, spotlight, or off.
  • Highlights the matching row in the side panel (or overlay).
  • Shows a tooltip with the hotspot label.

Pointing at a side-panel row triggers the same paired highlight in reverse — the row is the focus, and the matching hotspot on the image gets the highlight ring.

docs.title: Focus (keyboard)
docs.text:
Pressing Tab moves focus through interactive elements in this order:

  1. Diagram tabs (when multiple diagrams are attached).
  2. Each side-panel row, top to bottom.
  3. Inside an open popup: variant picker → quantity → add-to-cart.

Focus rings follow the theme's focus styles when present. Pressing Enter or Space on a focused side-panel row opens the popup just like a click.

docs.title: Click
docs.text:
Click any of:

  • A hotspot on the image.
  • A side-panel row.
  • A part thumbnail in an open popup.
  • A part bold title in an open popup.

Behavior:

Click target Result Hotspot on image Open popup Side-panel row Open popup Thumbnail in popup Navigate to product page (new tab) Bold title in popup Navigate to product page (new tab) Variant in popup picker Update the popup's price, stock, SKU live Quantity stepper Increment / decrement quantity (clamped to stock) Add to cart in popup Add to Shopify cart, show success, update header cart

docs.title: Touch
docs.text:
Mobile and tablet behave the same as click — tap on a hotspot to open the popup. Tap-and-hold on a hotspot doesn't trigger the popup; the shopper must tap to commit.

The popup is large enough to use comfortably with thumbs and uses sticky headers so the Add to cart button stays in reach while scrolling longer popup content.

docs.title: Variant change
docs.text:
When a hotspot is mapped to a product (not a specific variant) and the product has more than the auto-default variant:

  • The popup shows a variant picker (swatches if option names are Color / Size / similar; otherwise a dropdown).
  • Picking a different variant:
    • Updates the price (and the struck-through compare-at price if on sale).
    • Updates the SKU.
    • Updates the stock state — if the new variant is sold out, the add-to-cart and quantity controls disable.
    • Updates the swatch indicator.

The popup stays open during variant switches.

docs.title: Cart redirect
docs.text:
After Add to cart, the next behavior depends on the Cart redirect mode shop setting:

  • Stay on page (default) — popup stays open, cart count updates in the theme header.
  • Open cart drawer — Shopify's cart drawer opens (if your theme supports drawer carts).
  • Go to cart page — full navigation to /cart.

docs.title: Closing the popup
docs.text:
The popup closes when:

  • The shopper clicks outside it.
  • The shopper presses Esc.
  • The shopper clicks the × in the top-right corner.

Adding to cart does not close the popup automatically — shoppers often add multiple parts in sequence.

docs.title: Layouts
docs.text: The block supports three layouts. Pick one in Settings → Storefront.

docs.title: Side panel (default)
docs.text:
Diagram on the left, scrollable parts list on the right.

Best for:

  • Wide product pages — desktop-first stores, lots of horizontal space.
  • Diagrams with many parts (10+). The side panel scrolls independently so shoppers can browse without losing the diagram from view.

On narrow viewports (mobile), the side panel collapses below the diagram automatically. No separate mobile setting is required.

docs.title: Overlay
docs.text:
Parts list overlays the bottom or side of the diagram image, with hotspot click expanding inline detail. The overlay is partially translucent so the diagram remains visible behind it.

Best for:

  • Stores with narrow product pages or layouts where a separate side panel would overflow.
  • Diagrams with few parts (≤8) — the overlay style works best when the parts list stays short.

Click expands a detail card inline; the layout doesn't navigate the shopper away from the diagram view.

docs.title: Popup-only
docs.text:
Diagram only — no parts list at all. Each hotspot opens a popup on click with full detail.

Best for:

  • Minimalist storefronts where a side panel would feel heavy.
  • Diagrams used as a visual catalog navigator rather than a parts reference.

Shoppers must hover or click each hotspot to discover what it represents, so make sure your hotspot labels are descriptive — they appear in the hover tooltip.

docs.title: Comparing layouts at a glance
docs.text:
Side panel Overlay Popup-only Diagram visible on hotspot detail Yes Mostly No (popup covers) Side parts list always visible Yes Half-visible No Best on narrow screens OK (collapses) Better Best Best for many hotspots (10+) Best Cramped OK Discoverability without hover Best Good Lowest

Switch between them at any time in Storefront settings. Use Storefront preview to compare before publishing the change.

docs.title: Theme Editor
docs.text:
Everything you need to do inside Shopify's theme editor — which is intentionally minimal. Parts Diagram puts visual / interaction configuration in the embedded admin so the theme editor stays clean.

Page What it covers Add the block Drop the Parts Diagram block onto your product template API base URL The single setting in the block. When to leave blank, when to override Troubleshooting Block missing, doesn't render, wrong styling

docs.title: Why the theme editor only has one setting
docs.text:
In early versions, the theme editor block exposed every visual toggle (layout, hover effect, show prices, show SKU, etc.). This created two sources of truth — a merchant could change "Show prices" in the theme editor and see no effect, because the shop-level setting silently overrode it.

To eliminate that confusion, all visual / interaction config now lives exclusively in Settings → Storefront. The theme editor block exposes only the API base URL, which is a per-deployment infrastructure setting — see API base URL.

docs.title: Add the Parts Diagram Block
docs.text: The Parts Diagram block is a Shopify Theme App Extension. It works on Online Store 2.0 themes — every theme published in the Shopify Theme Store after mid-2021 qualifies.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. In your Shopify Admin, open Online Store → Themes.
  2. Find your active theme and click Customize.
  3. At the top of the customize editor, switch the template selector to Products → Default product (or the specific product template you use).
  4. In the section list on the left, find your Product information section.
  5. Click Add block under that section.
  6. Under the Apps group, choose Parts Diagram.
  7. Drag the new block into the position you want — most stores place it directly below the product gallery.
  8. Click Save at the top right.

The block now renders on every product page using that template.

docs.title: Multiple product templates
docs.text: If you have several product templates (for example, Default, Featured, Apparel), repeat the steps above for each template where you want the diagram to appear. Theme editor block placements are template-specific.

docs.title: Custom theme sections
docs.text:
Some themes allow apps to render in custom locations beyond the product information section. Parts Diagram is registered as a section block target — meaning it can be added to any section that declares "App blocks" support, on product templates only.

If your theme doesn't expose Add block under your product section, contact your theme developer or use a theme that supports app blocks.

docs.title: Removing the block
docs.text:
Open the same template in the customize editor, find the Parts Diagram block, click the menu next to it, and choose Remove block. Click Save.

Removing the block from the theme does not delete diagrams or assignments — it only stops them from rendering on the storefront. Re-add the block at any time to bring the diagrams back.

docs.title: API Base URL
docs.text: The single setting on the Parts Diagram theme block. Most stores leave it blank — and that's the right default.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text:
When the storefront block boots, it needs to reach the Parts Diagram API to load diagrams, hotspots, and live pricing.

  • Blank (default) — the block uses your store's Shopify App Proxy, which routes /apps/parts-diagram/* requests through Shopify to our API. No DNS or CORS configuration required.
  • Set to a custom URL — the block calls that URL directly instead. Use only if you have a dedicated API deployment (multi-environment staging, a private regional cluster, or a development tunnel).

For the overwhelming majority of stores, leave this blank.

docs.title: When to set it
docs.text:
You only need a custom value when:

  • You're testing a development build of the app on a sandbox store, and the developer points you to a temporary tunnel URL such as https://parts-diagram-dev-7.example.com.
  • You're piloting a private deployment of the app on infrastructure separate from the public production cluster.
  • You have a multi-region setup and want a specific store to pin to a specific region's API.

In all three cases, the URL will be provided to you by the development team or your account manager.

docs.title: How to set it
docs.text:
1. Open Online Store → Themes → Customize.
  2. Switch to the product template that has the Parts Diagram block.
  3. Click the Parts Diagram block in the section list.
  4. Paste the full URL into API base URL — including https://.
  5. Click Save.

Reload the storefront in a private window to verify. If the diagram loads, you're done.

docs.title: Reverting
docs.text: To go back to the App Proxy default, clear the field, click Save, reload the storefront in a private window. The block falls back to the standard /apps/parts-diagram/* proxy path.

docs.title: Common pitfalls
docs.text:
• HTTP not HTTPS — Shopify storefronts are HTTPS. An HTTP API URL will be blocked by mixed-content policy.
  • Trailing slash — both https://api.example.com and https://api.example.com/ work; don't include path segments.
  • Stale dev tunnels — development tunnel URLs change frequently. If your storefront stops loading after a development cycle, blank the field, save, and the block falls back to the App Proxy.

docs.title: Theme Editor Troubleshooting
docs.text: Common issues when adding or rendering the Parts Diagram block. For admin-side issues, see the global Troubleshooting.

docs.title: Block doesn't appear in "Add block" menu
docs.text:
Cause: Theme is not Online Store 2.0, or the section doesn't support app blocks.

  • Confirm your theme is OS 2.0. Go to Themes → click the active theme → if you see a dot-only badge in the customize editor, you're on OS 2.0. Themes from before mid-2021 are usually not.
  • Confirm you're inside a Product template — Parts Diagram only appears on product templates.
  • Confirm you're clicking Add block inside a section that supports app blocks. The product information section in OS 2.0 themes always does; custom sections may not.

If your theme doesn't qualify, you can create a new product template based on Dawn (Shopify's free reference theme) and assign it to the products that need diagrams.

docs.title: Block is added but shows nothing
docs.text:
A few likely causes:

  1. No diagram is assigned to the product. Open the embedded admin → Assignments → confirm a diagram is linked to this product.
  2. Custom API URL is wrong. If you've set a value for API base URL, blank it and save. The block then falls back to the App Proxy default.
  3. Browser blocks the request. Open browser DevTools → Console. Look for messages starting with [parts-diagram]. CORS or 4xx responses point at API/proxy issues; share them with support.
  4. Theme caches aggressively. Some themes inline the block via layout caching. After saving in the customize editor, reload the storefront in a private window.

docs.title: Hotspots are misaligned
docs.text:
The diagram image dimensions changed between when hotspots were drawn and now.

  • Open the diagram editor → check the image dimensions in the editor's title bar match the image you most recently uploaded.
  • If you uploaded a new image with different proportions, hotspots drawn on the old image won't fit. Either revert to the original image or redraw hotspots on the new one.

docs.title: Styling clashes with the theme
docs.text:
The block uses scoped CSS, but a few host-theme rules can leak in:

  • Font — the block inherits the theme's body font for headings and the parts list. If the theme's font has unusual letter spacing, labels may wrap unexpectedly. Adjust the labels to be shorter, or contact support to restrict label widths.
  • Colors — the block reads CSS custom properties exposed by Dawn and most modern themes for buttons and text colors. If the theme doesn't expose them, the block uses a neutral default.
  • Image gallery interference — themes that use carousels or overlays around the gallery may visually compete with the diagram. If clicks pass through the diagram, lower the diagram's z-index by reordering the section blocks.

If you suspect a theme-specific styling conflict, contact support with your theme name and a screenshot.

docs.title: "Block missing" warning on the dashboard
docs.text:
The embedded admin shows this warning when it can't find the Parts Diagram block on the active theme. Re-add the block following Add the Block, then click Re-check on the dashboard.

If you legitimately want diagrams hidden on the storefront for a period (catalog overhaul, branding refresh), simply leave the block out — the warning is informational, not an error.
